title: Attacks on Gulf energy facilities intensify, fueling fears of a 'gas war'
subhead: Tehran has vowed to wage a vast retaliation campaign against neighboring countries' energy infrastructure, after Iran's South Pars gas field was struck by the Israeli military.

Paris mayoral frontrunner Grégoire faces fierce attacks from rivals in debate
subhead: Emmanuel Grégoire, the Socialist who led in the first round, struggled to present his proposals during the debate as Sophia Chikirou and Rachida Dati took aim at him from his left and right.

title: Macron announces mediation to salvage European fighter jet project
subhead: The FCAS program, a flagship joint effort to build a next-generation combat aircraft between France, Germany and Spain, has faltered as disagreements persist between France's Dassault Aviation and Airbus.

title: French local elections: In Nice, right-wing leader's refusal to choose between the right and far right sows confusion
subhead: On Tuesday, Les Républicains party leader Bruno Retailleau refused to take a side between incumbent mayor Christian Estrosi, whom his party has endorsed, and his rival, Eric Ciotti, who is allied with the far right.

title: Yazidi genocide trial: Sabri Essid, the 'monster' husband who became a slave owner
subhead: On Wednesday, the French jihadist's ex-wife told the court about the man he had been before he turned to jihad. In France, she was the first woman to suffer his violence and perversion. Then, in Syria, the terrorist found the ideal environment to freely unleash his desire to dominate.

title: Mark Carney's delicate balancing act at Canada's helm
subhead: The pragmatic former banker captivated the world with his speech in Davos on the autonomy of middle powers in the face of hegemonic giants. But the political footing of this tightrope walker remains difficult to define.

title: Five takeaways from the first round of French local elections
subhead: The 2026 municipal elections have already concluded in the vast majority of French towns, with a mayor being directly elected in the first round. In larger cities, however, the right and the Socialists are in the lead for the second round.
